title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Model Release
description: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 4.6 on February 17, 2026, delivering Opus-level performance at Sonnet-level pricing ($3/$15 per million tokens). Features 1M token context window, ~75-79.6% SWE-bench score, 72.5% OSWorld score, and enhanced capabilities in coding, agent tasks, computer use, and long-context reasoning. Now the default model for free/Pro users.

community_feedback.quote: 70% of developers preferred Sonnet 4.6 over Sonnet 4.5; 59% preferred it over flagship Opus 4.5
1 million tokens? Finally found a model that can read my entire messy codebase without judging it
Sonnet 4.6 achieves near-Opus intelligence at lower cost - significant for budget-constrained teams
community_feedback.source: Developer community via Chinese tech blogs

community_feedback.quote: We originally expected Sonnet 4.6 to outperform Opus 4.5 in programming, but results show it's just a Cowork upgrade
Software stocks declined following release (Intuit -5%, Oracle -3%, Salesforce -2%, Adobe -1%)

title: Codex CLI Comprehensive Overhaul
description: OpenAI released major Codex updates on February 9, 2026: New GPT-5.2-Codex model with 40% faster reasoning, 400K token input capacity, support for 50+ programming languages, multi-agent parallel execution, and new macOS desktop application. Features include MCP protocol integration, Work Tree mechanism, double rate quotas, and automated workflow execution.

community_feedback.quote: Complex configuration requirements and output stability issues

title: Composer 1.5 Model Release
description: Cursor released Composer 1.5 on February 9, 2026, a self-developed AI programming model 4x faster than equivalents with 20x increased reinforcement learning training scale. Specialized for multi-file editing with project-level understanding, semantic search, and automatic dependency tracking. Most interactions complete within 30 seconds.

community_feedback.quote: Sitting there watching 8 AI agents race, you can smell the tokens burning furiously
Can consume significant tokens (thousands of tokens just to change a chart color when running multiple models)
Pro version at $16/month (60% more expensive than GitHub Copilot)

title: Gemini CLI v0.26.0 Release
description: Version 0.26.0 released around February 1, 2026. Building on v0.22.0 (January 2026) which opened Gemini 3 series models to free users, includes Conductor extension for active project management, Endor Labs security integration, audio/video processing capabilities, and MCP server management. Weekly release cadence with preview, stable, and nightly builds.
